questions.question: Which of the following is the highest peak in the Western Ghats and South India?
questions.explanation: Anamudi, standing at 2,695 meters, is located in the Eravikulam National Park in Kerala. It is the highest peak in both the Western Ghats and South India.

questions.question: The famous cold desert region 'Ladakh' lies between which two mountain ranges?
questions.explanation: The cold desert of Ladakh is positioned between the Ladakh range to the north and the Zanskar range to the south.

questions.question: The 'Duncan Passage' separates which of the following islands?
options: Rutland Island (South Andaman) and Little Andaman
questions.explanation: The Duncan Passage is a strait in the Indian Ocean, about 48 km wide, that separates Rutland Island (part of South Andaman) and Little Andaman to the south.
